The evil passions of the heart emerge in various forms; but the Lord looks at our motives, as well as our conduct. Those who deal cruelly will be treated cruelly. Other nations were held accountable for injuries done to people; Judah is held accountable for dishonor done to God. Judah despised the law of the Lord; and he justly gave them up to strong delusion; nor was it any excuse for their sin that they were following lies and idols, after which their fathers walked. The worst abominations and most grievous oppressions have been committed by some of those who claim to worship the Lord. Such conduct leads many to unbelief and vile idolatry.

We need often to be reminded of the mercies we have received; which adds much to the evil of the sins we have committed. They had help for their souls, which taught them how to make good use of their earthly possessions, and were therefore more valuable. Faithful ministers are great blessings to any people; but it is God who raises them up to be so. Sinners' own consciences will witness that he has not been wanting to them in the means of grace. They tried to lead believers aside. Satan and his agents are busy corrupting the minds of young people who look heavenward; they overcome many by drawing them to the love of mirth and pleasure, and into drinking company. Many young men who seemed promising as followers of religion have erred through strong drink, and have been ruined forever. The Lord complains of sin, especially the sins of his professing people, as a burden to him. And though his patience is exhausted, his power is not, and so the sinner will find to their cost. When people reject God’s word, adding stubbornness to sin, and this becomes the general character of a people, they will be given up to misery, despite all their boasted power and resources. May we then humble ourselves before the Lord for all our ingratitude and unfaithfulness.
